description: Lunarae Zimbabwe Customs SI Reference Document – comprehensive summary of all applicable Statutory Instruments for the AI system prompt

statutory_instruments.title: Control of Goods (OGIL) Standards Assessment – CBCA Notice 2024
statutory_instruments.summary: Establishes Consignment Based Conformity Assessment (CBCA) programme. All goods in Fourth Schedule with FOB > USD 1,000 require Certificate of Conformity from appointed Conformity Assessment Body before customs clearance.
key_rules: First Schedule goods (motor vehicles HS 87.01-87.07, 87.16) – CBCA required REGARDLESS of value. No FOB threshold.
Fourth Schedule goods – CBCA required if FOB > USD 1,000 per consignment.
Assessment conducted in country of origin by appointed Conformity Assessment Body.
Goods arriving without CoC: compulsory destination assessment + 12% CIF penalty to Treasury.
Non-conforming goods must be re-exported or destroyed at importer's cost.
UN organisations and Diplomatic Missions EXEMPT (Vienna Convention).
Assessment fees: Motor vehicles USD 140-200 per unit; General goods 0.25-0.50% FOB (min USD 240, max USD 2,675).
Destination assessment fees same as above plus 12% CIF penalty.

statutory_instruments.title: Control of Goods (Import & Export) (Commerce) (Amendment) Regulations, 2024 (No. 10)
statutory_instruments.summary: Prohibits import of second-hand motor vehicles aged 10 years and above from date of manufacture. Consolidated and expanded by SI 59/2026.
key_rules: Second-hand vehicles aged ≥ 10 years from date of manufacture: PROHIBITED for import.
Prohibited vehicles must be re-exported by owner at own expense.

statutory_instruments.title: Control of Goods (Import & Export) (Commerce) (Amendment) Regulations, 2026 (No. 15)
statutory_instruments.summary: Comprehensive consolidation of all import/export licensing requirements. Introduces licence fees, rejection grounds, second-hand clothing prohibition, expanded goods lists.
key_rules: Import licence application fee: USD 100 non-refundable (or ZiG equivalent at interbank rate). SI 59/2026 s5B.
Export licence application fee: USD 50 non-refundable (or ZiG equivalent). SI 59/2026 s5B.
Grounds for rejection: goods not meeting quality/safety standards; prejudicial to economic interests; applicant previously contravened regulations. SI 59/2026 s5A.

Second-hand vehicles ≥ 10 years PROHIBITED. If not re-exported within 60 days → forfeited to State. Exceptions: deceased estates, immigrants (C&E Act s9NO(1)/s105(1)), diplomats returning from postings, vintage vehicles. SI 59/2026 s7A.
Second-hand CLOTHING: PROHIBITED except with permit for charitable purposes only. SI 59/2026 s7B(1).
Second-hand UNDERGARMENTS: STRICTLY PROHIBITED under ALL circumstances. SI 59/2026 s7B(2).
Offences: fine up to level 12 OR imprisonment up to 1 year, or both.

Individuals may import limited personal use quantities monthly without licence (cooking oil 4L, sugar 4kg, cereals 2kg, jam 2kg, peanut butter 2kg, margarine 2kg, laundry soap 1 box/24 bars, washing powder 4kg, 1 blanket, 4 pieces cotton woven fabric, body creams 1 case/6). SI 59/2026 Third Schedule Part II.

import_licence_goods_schedule.note: See First Schedule. Key categories requiring import licence:
categories: Specific pharma (antacids, aspirin, ibuprofen, paracetamol, metformin, metronidazole, cotrimoxazole, erythromycin, amoxicillin, cloxacillin, IV fluids) HS 3004.xx

statutory_instruments.title: Presidential Powers (Temporary Measures) (Zimbabwe Gold Notes and Coins) Regulations, 2024
statutory_instruments.summary: Establishes Zimbabwe Gold (ZiG) as the official domestic currency backed by gold and foreign exchange reserves. ZiG is legal tender alongside USD.
key_rules: ZiG is legal tender in all transactions alongside USD and other prescribed currencies (Reserve Bank Act s44A).
Initial ZiG value: equivalent to 1 milligram of 99% gold at LBMA spot price. Thereafter determined by inflation differential and gold/mineral reserve movements.
All Zimbabwe dollar balances converted to ZiG on effective date at formula A/B.
ZiG notes: 1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100, 200 ZiG denominations. All cotton paper, 155mm x 65mm.
For customs: all duties payable in ZiG at prevailing RBZ interbank rate, or in USD.
Customs duty formula unchanged; ZiG conversion applies at point of payment.

statutory_instruments.title: Customs and Excise (Suspension) (Amendment) Regulations, 2024 (No. 275)
statutory_instruments.summary: Wholly suspends customs duty on imported fertilisers for approved importers for the 2024/2025 season.
key_rules: Duty 100% suspended on: Urea (3102.10.00, 3102.80.00) up to 100,000 metric tonnes; Ammonium nitrate (3102.30.00) up to 150,000 metric tonnes.
Only available to importers approved AND licensed by Ministry of Agriculture (in consultation with Ministry of Industry & Commerce and Fertiliser Manufacturing Industry).
Commissioner must verify compliance with Revenue Authority Act s34C (tax clearance).
Importers selling suspended-duty fertiliser at prices equal to or higher than duty-paid fertiliser → liable to pay suspended duty + applicable penalties.
Approved importers may also exhaust ring-fenced allocations from SI 166/2023.

statutory_instruments.title: Base Minerals Export Control (Unbénéficiated Base Mineral Ores) Order, 2023
statutory_instruments.replaces: Base Minerals Export Control (Iron-Bearing Ores) Order, 2022
statutory_instruments.summary: Prohibits export of any unbénéficiated base mineral ore from Zimbabwe without written ministerial permit.
key_rules: No unbénéficiated base mineral ore may be exported without written permit from Minister of Mines and Mining Development.
Permit granted only if: goods cannot be processed in Zimbabwe; or sample for assay/testing (limited quantity).
Base minerals = coal + all other minerals/substances including coke, slimes, concentrates, slags, tailings (excludes precious metals/stones/mineral oils/gas).
Unbénéficiated = any ore that has NOT undergone processing within Zimbabwe to any extent.
Penalty: fine up to value of the base minerals involved + imprisonment.
statutory_instruments.covered_headings: HS chapters 26 (metallic ores), 27 (coal, coke), and relevant metals chapters (72, 74, 75, 76, 78, 79, 80)

statutory_instruments.title: Control of Goods (OGIL) (Amendment) Notice, 2024 (No. 16)
statutory_instruments.summary: Removes certain goods from import/export licence requirements, adding them to Open General Import Licence (OGIL). Also adjusts export licence fees.
key_rules: DEREGULATED (no longer need import licence): Cables (HS 76.14, 85.44); Binders/twine/ropes/cables (HS 56.07, 56.08, 56.09); Luggage/trunks/handbags (HS 42.02); Wiring harness (HS 85.44); Woven fabric (HS 5208-5211); Cooler boxes (HS 9403700).
DEREGULATED from export licence: Gypsum (HS 2520.10); Bin liners (HS 39.20, 39.21).
Export licence fee: USD 50 payable in local currency at prevailing rate.

statutory_instruments.title: Customs and Excise (Surtax Tariff) (Amendment) Notice, 2025 (No. 6)
statutory_instruments.summary: Introduces Second Schedule to Surtax Tariff with 30% surtax on specified imports. Removes yoghurt from First Schedule surtax.
key_rules: 30% surtax ad valorem charged IN ADDITION to normal customs duty on Second Schedule goods.
Yoghurt (0403.20.00) removed from First Schedule surtax (no longer surtaxed).

30% SURTAX applies to: Milk 0401.10/20; Yoghurt 0403.20; Cane sugar 1701.13/14/99; Beer 2203.00; White cement 2523.21; Other cement 2523.29; Ceramic tiles 6907.21/22/23; Flat-rolled steel 7211.19; Steel bars/rods 7214.20/30/99; Steel angle sections 7216.10/21/22.

statutory_instruments.title: Customs and Excise (Designated Deemed Smuggled Goods) Regulations, 2025
statutory_instruments.summary: Designates commercial goods where failure to produce customs clearance documentary evidence results in deemed smuggling liability.
key_rules: Manufacturers, wholesalers, retailers AND individuals in possession of designated goods must produce authentic documentary evidence that duty was properly paid/cleared.
Failure to produce evidence = DEEMED SMUGGLED → liable for duty + applicable penalties.
NOTE: Customers buying from local retailers are EXEMPT from producing proof (responsibility rests with importer/manufacturer/wholesaler/retailer).
Does NOT apply to goods purchased locally from local manufacturers.

statutory_instruments.title: Customs and Excise (General) (Amendment) Regulations, 2025 (No. 124)
statutory_instruments.summary: Grants rebate of customs duty on equipment and machinery for setting up solar-powered EV charging stations, for ZERA-approved operators.
key_rules: Duty rebate available to ZERA-approved operators importing equipment solely for construction/erection of solar-powered EV charging stations.
Commissioner must verify compliance with Revenue Authority Act s34C.
Equipment cannot be transferred between operators/sites without Commissioner's written consent.
Equipment cannot be sold/disposed without Commissioner's written permission + payment of original duty at time of entry.
Importer must declare equipment will be used solely for the approved charging station.
Rebate covers: solar panels, inverters, EV chargers, battery storage systems, charging control units, mounting structures, electrical wiring components, monitoring systems, grounding equipment.

duty_calculation_rules.formula: Customs Duty = CIF Value × Duty Rate
duty_calculation_rules.surtax_formula: Surtax Duty = CIF Value × Surtax Rate (30%); Surtax goods: total duty = customs duty + surtax + VAT on (CIF + customs duty + surtax)
duty_calculation_rules.currency: Duties calculated in USD. Payable in USD or ZiG at prevailing RBZ interbank rate.
notes: CIF must be allocated proportionally across line items by FOB value when freight/insurance covers multiple goods.
For vehicles: always check second-hand age (10yr ban) and CBCA requirement regardless of value.
For pharmaceuticals: check if specific pharma on SI 59/2026 First Schedule (requires import licence from Ministry of Industry & Commerce, distinct from MCAZ requirement).
Fertiliser: check if duty suspension applies (approved importer with Agriculture Ministry licence).

vehicles_new.notes: CBCA fee USD 140/unit (rest of world), USD 200/unit (UK)

vehicles_secondhand.notes: Exceptions for deceased estates, immigrants, diplomats, vintage vehicles

clothing_new.cbca: Required if FOB > USD 1,000 (SI 35/2024 Fourth Schedule headings 61-62)
clothing_new.import_licence: Required for school uniforms. General clothing per SI 59/2026 First Schedule.

clothing_secondhand.import_licence: PROHIBITED except charitable permit. Undergarments: ABSOLUTELY PROHIBITED (SI 59/2026 s7B)

pharmaceuticals_broad.import_licence: Most HS 3004 items require import licence from Ministry of Industry & Commerce per SI 59/2026
pharmaceuticals_broad.authority: Ministry of Industry & Commerce (import licence) + MCAZ (medicine registration – separate requirement)

cooking_oil.personal_exemption: 4 litres/month without licence for personal use

fertiliser.duty_suspension: Available for Ministry of Agriculture approved importers (SI 178/2024)

base_minerals_ores.notes: Applies to all unbeneficiated ores. Only exceptions: genuine technical inability to process locally, or assay samples
